steps.title: Identify the horizontal asymptote
steps.content: For the exponential function $f(x) = ab^x + c$, the horizontal asymptote is the line $y = c$. Reading from the graph, the curve approaches a horizontal line at approximately $y = -5$. Therefore, $c \approx -5$.

steps.title: Observe that all function values are below the x-axis
steps.content: The horizontal asymptote is at $y = -5$, which lies below the $x$-axis. The graph shows that the entire curve is below this asymptote, meaning all function values satisfy $f(x) < -5 < 0$.

steps.title: Count the number of zeros
steps.content: Since $f(x) < 0$ for all $x$, the curve never crosses or touches the $x$-axis (where $y = 0$). Therefore, there are zero values of $x$ where $f(x) = 0$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
